description: Add unit tests for the API Gateway authorizer injection in the RestApi construct, covering Cognito, JWT, and no-auth scenarios.
steps: Create test/constructs/rest-api.test.ts following the pattern in test/constructs/graphql.test.ts: mock LambdaFunction to avoid esbuild bundling, create a minimal OpenAPI spec inline (yaml dump to temp file), instantiate RestApi with different authentication configurations.

Test case 1 - Cognito authentication: Create a RestApi with ICognitoAuthentication (mock IUserPool). Use Template.fromStack() assertions to verify the SpecRestApi's Body property includes securityDefinitions with x-amazon-apigateway-authorizer of type cognito_user_pools and the correct providerARNs.

Test case 2 - JWT authentication: Create a RestApi with IJwtAuthentication ({ issuerUrl, audience, jwksUrl }). Assert the template includes an additional Lambda function for the authorizer and the spec includes a token-type authorizer in securityDefinitions.
Test case 3 - No authentication: Create a RestApi with no authentication prop. Assert no securityDefinitions or authorizer resources appear in the synthesized template.

Test case 4 - Verify security is applied per-operation: Create a RestApi with authentication and a spec that has global security set. Assert the synthesized spec has security on each operation (patchSecurity still works correctly with the new scheme).
acceptance_criteria: All new tests in test/constructs/rest-api.test.ts pass
Tests verify the Cognito authorizer configuration in the OpenAPI spec
Tests verify the JWT Lambda authorizer configuration in the OpenAPI spec
Tests verify backward compatibility when no auth is configured
